auth.description:
Authenticate via service account from https://console.cloud.google.com/ > IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Create Service Account > Keys > Add key.

You can optionally use domain-wide delegation (https://support.google.com/a/answer/162106?hl=en#zippy=%2Cset-up-domain-wide-delegation-for-a-client) to access calendars without adding the service account to each one.

**Note:** Without a user email, the service account only has access to calendars you explicitly share with it.

userEmail.description: Email address of the user to impersonate for domain-wide delegation.

userEmail.description: Email address of the user to impersonate for domain-wide delegation.

create_meet_link.description: Automatically create a Google Meet video conference link for this event

description.description: Description of the event. You can use HTML tags here.

end_date_time.description: By default it'll be 30 min post start time
end_date_time.displayName: End date time of the event

start_date_time.displayName: Start date time of the event

auth.description:
Authenticate via service account from https://console.cloud.google.com/ > IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Create Service Account > Keys > Add key.

You can optionally use domain-wide delegation (https://support.google.com/a/answer/162106?hl=en#zippy=%2Cset-up-domain-wide-delegation-for-a-client) to access calendars without adding the service account to each one.

userEmail.description: Email address of the user to impersonate for domain-wide delegation.

event_id.description: The unique ID of the event (e.g., "abc123def456"). You can find this in the event URL or from other calendar actions.

max_attendees.description: Maximum number of attendees to include in the response. If there are more attendees, only the participant is returned.

time_zone.description: Time zone for the response (e.g., "America/New_York", "Europe/London"). Defaults to the calendar's time zone if not specified.

singleEvents.description: Whether to expand recurring events into instances and only return single one-off events and instances of recurring events, but not the underlying recurring events themselves.

specific_event.description: Enable to monitor a specific event instead of all events in the calendar.

event_starts_in.description: Fires at a specified amount of time before an event starts (e.g., a reminder).

auth.description:
Authenticate via service account from https://console.cloud.google.com/ > IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Create Service Account > Keys > Add key.

You can optionally use domain-wide delegation (https://support.google.com/a/answer/162106?hl=en#zippy=%2Cset-up-domain-wide-delegation-for-a-client) to access calendars without adding the service account to each one.

auth.description:
Authenticate via service account from https://console.cloud.google.com/ > IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Create Service Account > Keys > Add key.

You can optionally use domain-wide delegation (https://support.google.com/a/answer/162106?hl=en#zippy=%2Cset-up-domain-wide-delegation-for-a-client) to access calendars without adding the service account to each one.

**Note:** Without a user email, the service account only has access to calendars you explicitly share with it.

userEmail.description: Email address of the user to impersonate for domain-wide delegation.

specific_event.description: Enable to monitor a specific event instead of all events in the calendar.

time_value.description: The amount of time before the event starts.

new_calendar.description: Fires when a new calendar is created or becomes accessible.

access_role_filter.description: Only trigger for calendars with specific access roles (optional)

calendar_name_filter.description: Only trigger for calendars containing this text in name or description (optional)

exclude_shared.description: Only trigger for calendars you own, not shared calendars

new_event.description: Fires when a new event is created in a calendar.

auth.description:
Authenticate via service account from https://console.cloud.google.com/ > IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Create Service Account > Keys > Add key.

You can optionally use domain-wide delegation (https://support.google.com/a/answer/162106?hl=en#zippy=%2Cset-up-domain-wide-delegation-for-a-client) to access calendars without adding the service account to each one.

**Note:** Without a user email, the service account only has access to calendars you explicitly share with it.

userEmail.description: Email address of the user to impersonate for domain-wide delegation.

event_types.description: Filter by specific event types (leave empty to monitor all event types)

search_filter.description: Only trigger for events containing this text in title, description, or location (optional)

new_event_matching_search.description: Fires when a new event is created that matches a specified search term.

auth.description:
Authenticate via service account from https://console.cloud.google.com/ > IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Create Service Account > Keys > Add key.

You can optionally use domain-wide delegation (https://support.google.com/a/answer/162106?hl=en#zippy=%2Cset-up-domain-wide-delegation-for-a-client) to access calendars without adding the service account to each one.

**Note:** Without a user email, the service account only has access to calendars you explicitly share with it.

userEmail.description: Email address of the user to impersonate for domain-wide delegation.

search_fields.description: Specify which fields to search in (leave empty to use Google's default search across all fields)

search_term.description: The keyword(s) to search for in new events (searches across title, description, location, and attendees by default).

new_or_updated_event.description: Triggers when an event is added or updated

expandRecurringEvent.description: If true, the trigger will activate for every occurrence of a recurring event.
